channel.title: New York on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog
channel.description: Recent content in New York on Lucky Taorem | Tech & AI Blog

item.title: New York Enacts Data Center Moratorium
item.description:
Introduction to Data Center Moratorium  

New York Governor Kathy Hochul has signed an executive order enacting a one-year pause on the development of hyperscale data centers, marking the nation’s first statewide data center moratorium. This move is aimed at addressing the environmental and energy impact of these massive facilities. As reported earlier, the moratorium is a significant step towards regulating the data center industry.

item.title: New York Enacts Data Center Moratorium
item.description:
Introduction to Data Center Moratorium  

New York has taken a significant step towards regulating the growth of hyperscale data centers in the state. Governor Kathy Hochul has enacted a data center moratorium, which blocks new environmental permits for hyperscale data centers over 50 megawatts. This move is expected to give the state time to come up with regulations needed to protect residents from rising energy prices and environmental impact.
